title: Nursery Rhyme. CCCCXLVIII. Love And Matrimony.
content:
A cow and a calf,
            An ox and a half,
        Forty good shillings and three;
            Is that not enough tocher
            For a shoe-maker's daughter,
        A bonny lass with a black e'e?
meaning:
Unknown's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Nursery Rhyme. CCCCXLVIII. Love And Matrimony."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
